ucs.title: [LLM] Stealth Falcon (FruityArmor) PowerShell scheduled-task BITS backdoor C2
ucs.description: Hunts Stealth Falcon's signature implant chain: a scheduled task launches a PowerShell loader that uses the Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS) — via bitsadmin.exe or the BITS COM interface in PS — to beacon and exfiltrate. ESET and Citizen Lab attribute this exact 'Win32/StealthFalcon' chain (schtasks → powershell → BITS) to G0038 against UAE dissidents and journalists.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.parent_process) as parents values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="svchost.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="taskeng.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="schedule.dll") (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*Start-BitsTransfer*" OR Processes.process="*Microsoft.BackgroundIntelligentTransfer*" OR Processes.process="*[Reflection.Assembly]::Load*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-encodedcommand*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*DownloadString*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="bitsadmin.exe" (Processes.process="*/transfer*" OR Processes.process="*/addfile*" OR Processes.process="*/setnotifycmdline*") by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename count as bitsadmin_count] | where isnotnull(bitsadmin_count) OR match(cmds,"(?i)BitsTransfer|BackgroundIntelligentTransfer") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user parent_process_name process_name cmds bitsadmin_count
ucs.kql:
let timeWindow = 7d;
let schedTaskPS = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(timeWindow)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("svchost.exe","taskeng.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("Schedule","-k netsvcs")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Start-BitsTransfer","BackgroundIntelligentTransfer","FromBase64String","-enc ","-EncodedCommand","IEX","Reflection.Assembly")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessId, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let bitsChild = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(timeWindow)
| where FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/transfer","/addfile","/SetNotifyCmdLine","/create")
| project bitsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, bitsCmd=ProcessCommandLine, bitsParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
schedTaskPS
| join kind=leftouter (bitsChild) on DeviceId
| where isnotempty(bitsCmd) or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Start-BitsTransfer","BackgroundIntelligentTransfer")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(timeWindow)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("svchost.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe")
    | where RemotePort in (80,443) and not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
    | summarize beacons=dcount(RemoteIP), urls=make_set(RemoteUrl,20) by DeviceId
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, bitsCmd, beacons, urls
ucs.rationale: The schtasks→svchost/taskeng→powershell→BITS sequence is a near-unique Stealth Falcon fingerprint documented by ESET (Win32/StealthFalcon, 2019) and Citizen Lab (2016). Legitimate admin use of BITS rarely originates from a scheduled-task-spawned PowerShell process with encoded commands, so chaining the parent (Schedule service host) + PS BITS API + outbound HTTPS to non-corporate IPs collapses FP

ucs.title: [LLM] Stealth Falcon Windows Credential Manager & DPAPI vault harvesting
ucs.description: Hunts G0038's post-compromise credential theft step: direct file-system access to the Credential Manager store (Microsoft\Credentials) and DPAPI master keys (Microsoft\Protect) by non-Microsoft processes — the exact T1555.003/T1555.004 behaviour Citizen Lab observed Stealth Falcon performing against Emirati activists to pivot into webmail and cloud accounts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\Credentials\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Credentials\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\Protect\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Vault\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Login Data" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Cookies" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Local State") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where NOT match(process_name,"(?i)^(svchost|lsass|explorer|chrome|msedge|firefox|opera|brave|outlook|teams|onedrive|searchindexer|RuntimeBroker|backgroundTaskHost)\.exe$") | join type=outer dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as process_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" OR Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where isnotnull(process_cmd) OR match(process_name,"(?i)(powershell|wmic|rundll32|regsvr32)\.exe$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user process_name process_cmd paths
ucs.kql:
let lol = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe"]);
let allow = dynamic(["svchost.exe","lsass.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","onedrive.exe","searchindexer.exe","runtimebroker.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe","credentialuibroker.exe"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed","FileAccessed")
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\Credentials","\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Credentials","\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\Protect","\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Vault")
   or FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Local State","Web Data")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (allow)
| extend isLOLBin = InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (lol)
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ (lol)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("CredentialManager","Vault","DPAPI","CryptUnprotectData","Microsoft\\Protect","Microsoft\\Credentials","Login Data","masterkey","vaultcli")
    | project DeviceId, susPid=ProcessId, susCmd=ProcessCommandLine, susTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, susTime)) < 600
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, FileName, susCmd
ucs.rationale: Stealth Falcon explicitly targets the Windows DPAPI vault and Credential Manager stores to lift webmail/cloud creds for follow-on surveillance of activists — Citizen Lab and ESET both highlight this distinction from typical Mimikatz LSASS dumping (which most APTs use). Pairing direct AppData\Microsoft\Protect|Credentials access by a LOLBin (PowerShell/WMIC/rundll32) with a co-temporal command line
